description.p: Earning their name from the toll taken when attempting to pass them in combat, Toll Sentinels are bulwarks dedicated to protecting their allies and staying alive. Being the only pledge trained in heavy armour and the usage of shields, Toll sentinels have adapted their leap to be a defensive tool, controlling the battlefield through sheer force of will to hold position. A Toll Sentinel will always stay alive. After all, if you aren’t dead, your enemy hasn’t won yet.
Members of the Toll Sentinel Pledge have access to a defensive pool of weapons. Choose one weapon from the list below or a weapon from the base Wyrmslayer Weapons and become proficient with it.

tr.td: Critical hits against you become a normal hit instead whilst wielding this weapon.
Either singular or a pair, these large wings wrap around the wielder and their allies, shielding them from harm from all directions.
This weapon deals double damage to structures and constructs.
fist weapons, hammers, mauls, clubs, maces

When you make a weapon attack whilst holding this shield, the target make a CHA saving throw against your save DC or have disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you until the end of your next turn.

You can use your attack action to instead prepare yourself for a parry. Whilst in parry mode, ranged projectiles that hit allied targets (including you) within 5ft of you are instead deflected back at the attacker, dealing (1d6 + your attack modifier) piercing damage to them instead.

description.p: Formidable and untouchable, a Toll Sentinel stands tall. At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in heavy armour and shields.

A powerful aura radiates from you as you prepare to protect those who need it on the battlefield. At 3rd level, as a bonus action you can consume the use of your Enhanced Leap and take a defensive stance, rooting yourself in place and reducing your movement to 0. Whilst in this stance, you gain temporary hit points equal to your Leap Damage Die and your attack range increases to a minimum of 10ft, regardless of which weapon you are using. In addition, you and all allies within 10ft of you gain a +1 bonus to their AC and you can now use Heroic Leap as a reaction. You can still attack as normal whilst your Defensive Stance is active and you can choose to end it at any point during your turn. You cannot use Leap Strike whilst in your Defensive Stance.

sheet.description: As a bonus action you can consume the use of your Enhanced Leap and take a defensive stance, rooting yourself in place and reducing your movement to 0. Whilst in this stance, you gain {{leap-strike:damage}} temporary hit points and your attack range increases to a minimum of 10ft, regardless of which weapon you are using. In addition, you and all allies within 10ft of you gain a +1 bonus to their AC and you can now use Heroic Leap as a reaction. You can still attack as normal whilst your Defensive Stance is active and you can choose to end it at any point during your turn. You cannot use Leap Strike whilst in your Defensive Stance.

description.p: Starting at 3rd level, as a reaction whilst in your Defensive Stance, when a creature within your leap distance takes damage, you can use your enhanced leap to immediately leap to their side. Roll your Leap Strike damage die and reduce the creature’s damage taken by the amount rolled. The chosen creature must have a free space within 5ft of them to use this feature. When you move this way, you retain your Defensive Aura. At 15th level, you can roll your Leap Die twice for the damage reduction. Note that if you’ve already used Enhanced Leap on your turn, you cannot use Heroic Leap.

sheet.description: As a reaction whilst in your Defensive Stance, when a creature within your leap distance takes damage, you can use your enhanced leap to immediately leap to their side. Roll your Leap Strike damage die (d{{leap-strike:damage}}) and reduce the creature’s damage taken by the amount rolled. The chosen creature must have a free space within 5ft of them to use this feature. When you move this way, you retain your Defensive Aura. At 15th level, you can roll your Leap Die twice for the damage reduction. Note that if you’ve already used Enhanced Leap on your turn, you cannot use Heroic Leap.

description.p: You can assist your closest allies in their struggles and hardships. Starting at 7th level, whilst in your Defensive Stance, you and all allies within your 10ft radius have advantage on their strength and dexterity saving throws.

sheet.description: Whilst in your Defensive Stance, you and all allies within your 10ft radius have advantage on their strength and dexterity saving throws.

description.p: Starting at 10th level, when you deal damage to a creature using the attack action or Cataclysmic impact, you can push them 5ft in a direction of your choosing, regardless of their size. If you’re making an attack using Enhanced Leap, you can increase this distance to 10ft. Additionally, when you make an attack with Leap Strike, you can enter your Defensive Stance.

sheet.description: When you deal damage to a creature using the attack action or Cataclysmic impact, you can push them 5ft in a direction of your choosing, regardless of their size. If you’re making an attack using Enhanced Leap, you can increase this distance to 10ft. Additionally, when you make an attack with Leap Strike, you can enter your Defensive Stance.

description.p: At 15th level, whilst in your Defensive Stance, allies within your 10ft radius gain your current Draconic Resilience. In addition, when an enemy enters your Defensive Stance radius or starts their turn there, they must succeed on a charisma save against your Wyrmslayer save DC (8 + your proficiency + your Constitution modifier) or become frightened of you until the end of their next turn.

sheet.description: Whilst in your Defensive Stance, allies within your 10ft radius gain your current Draconic Resilience. In addition, when an enemy enters your Defensive Stance radius or starts their turn there, they must succeed on a DC {{cataclysmic-impact:dc}} charisma save or become frightened of you until the end of their next turn.

description.p: Starting at 18th, you can use Heroic Leap regardless of whether you are in your Defensive Stance or not. When you use Heroic Leap this way, you can immediately enter Defensive Stance. In addition when an ally within your Defensive Stance range takes damage, you can nullify all of their damage taken this round if they stay in the stance radius, as well as any negative effects that would afflict them. Once you use this feature, you must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

sheet.description: You can use Heroic Leap regardless of whether you are in your Defensive Stance or not. When you use Heroic Leap this way, you can immediately enter Defensive Stance. In addition when an ally within your Defensive Stance range takes damage, you can nullify all of their damage taken this round if they stay in the stance radius, as well as any negative effects that would afflict them.

description.p: An enormous shield that covers the wielders entire body from one side. Its sheer weight makes it a decent weapon. Critical hits against you become a normal hit instead whilst wielding this weapon.

element.name: Weapon Proficiency (Saviour Wings)
element.supports: Weapon, Melee, ID_INTERNAL_DAMAGE_TYPE_SLASHING, ID_INTERNAL_WEAPON_PROPERTY_TWOHANDED, ID_PW_WS_WEAPON_PROPERTY_PUSH, ID_PW_WS_WEAPON_PROPERTY_SHIELD, ID_PW_WS_WEAPON_PROPERTY_SEIGE, defenders-arsenal

description.p: Either singular or a pair, these large wings wrap around the wielder and their allies, shielding them from harm from all directions. You gain 15ft increased movement speed when moving towards allies within 200ft. Allied creatures excluding receive +1 AC whilst standing within 5ft of you when you wield this weapon.

element.name: Weapon Proficiency (Thunder Vambrace)
element.supports: Weapon, Melee, ID_INTERNAL_DAMAGE_TYPE_BLUDGEONING, ID_INTERNAL_WEAPON_PROPERTY_TWOHANDED, ID_PW_WS_WEAPON_PROPERTY_PUSH, ID_PW_WS_WEAPON_PROPERTY_SEIGE, defenders-arsenal

description.p: A large singular gauntlet that covers the entire arm. It’s thunderous power can push and break both objects and people with ease. This weapon deals double damage to structures and constructs.

A circular shield with a pair of horrific dragon eyes. Their otherworldly power goads an enemies gaze. When you make a weapon attack whilst holding this shield, the target make a CHA saving throw against your save DC or have disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you until the end of your next turn.

element.name: Weapon Proficiency (Deflector's Pledge)
element.supports: Weapon, Melee, ID_INTERNAL_DAMAGE_TYPE_BLUDGEONING, ID_INTERNAL_WEAPON_PROPERTY_TWOHANDED, ID_PW_WS_WEAPON_PROPERTY_SHIELD, ID_INTERNAL_WEAPON_PROPERTY_FINESSE, defenders-arsenal

description.p: A valiant kite shield emblazoned with a dragon motif. One handed, yet requiring both arms to posture and balance correctly. This shield reflects attacking missiles back with ease. You can use your attack action to instead prepare yourself for a parry. Whilst in parry mode, ranged projectiles that hit allied targets (including you) within 5ft of you are instead deflected back at the attacker, dealing (1d6 + your attack modifier) piercing damage to them instead.
